key_insight: In hydrated Rb2Ti2O5, water spontaneously self-organizes into one-dimensional double chains along the b axis, creating a structural pathway for fast proton transport.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a crystal-orientation-dependent strategy for designing solid proton conductors with high room-temperature conductivity and controllable hydration-driven performance.
method: Scanning electron microscopy to identify hydration-dependent 1D patterns and exfoliated surfaces
Density Functional Theory simulations to resolve ordered water arrangements and hydrogen bonding
Infrared spectroscopy to support the presence of densely packed water chains at higher hydration
evidence_signal: The abstract reports anisotropic superionic conductivity reaching 3 mS/cm at room temperature along b after hydration, consistent with the proposed Grotthuss-like transport mechanism.
watch_item: The conductivity and structure appear highly sensitive to hydration level and crystal orientation, so reproducibility and stability under operating humidity must be carefully controlled.
paper_title: One-dimensional self-organization of water molecules in proton conducting Andersson-Wadsley titanates
